phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest macro and global liquidity data relevant to Bitcoin. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report: - Fed current policy stance and next meeting rate-cut probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - DXY (US Dollar Index): current level, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and trend context — is the DXY strengthening (headwind for BTC) or weakening (tailwind for BTC)? - US 10-Year Treasury Yield: current level and recent directional trend — are yields rising (risk-off pressure, potential BTC headwind) or falling (liquidity easing, potential BTC tailwind)? - Global M2 Liquidity trend: is global central bank money supply (M2) expanding or contracting? Cite the most recent data or analyst commentary available. Expanding M2 = historically bullish for BTC; contracting M2 = historically bearish. - Any recent Fed speaker comments or FOMC minutes remarks in the last 48 hours that could shift rate expectations - Overall fiat liquidity environment assessment: Loose / Neutral / Tight ⚠ STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULE: Do NOT fabricate exact figures. If a specific data point is unavailable, state "Data Unavailable" explicitly rather than estimating. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [hawkish/dovish/neutral] | Next Meeting Cut Probability: [X%] - DXY: [level] | Today's Move: [Up/Down X%] | Trend: [Strengthening/Weakening] | BTC Impact: [Headwind/Tailwind/Neutral] - US 10Y Yield: [%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] | BTC Impact: [Headwind/Tailwind/Neutral] - Global M2 Liquidity: [Expanding/Contracting/Flat] | Source/Date: [cite source] - Key Fed Speaker Note: [most relevant comment in 48h, or "None"] - Fiat Liquidity Environment: [Loose/Neutral/Tight] - Macro Bias for BTC: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - MACRO & LIQUIDITY] - Fed Stance: [hawkish/dovish/neutral] | Cut Probability: [X%] - DXY: [level] | Move: [direction %] | BTC Impact: [Headwind/Tailwind/Neutral] - US 10Y Yield: [%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] | BTC Impact: [Headwind/Tailwind/Neutral] - Global M2: [Expanding/Contracting/Flat] - Key Fed Note: [comment or "None"] - Fiat Liquidity Environment: [Loose / Neutral / Tight] - Core Logic: [why this macro/liquidity setup matters for BTC today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report: - US Spot Bitcoin ETF net flows from the last 48 hours (total net inflow or outflow in USD). Break down by major issuers if available (e.g., BlackRock iShares IBIT, Fidelity FBTC, Ark 21Shares ARKB). Are the flows accelerating or decelerating? - CME Bitcoin Futures Open Interest (OI): current level in USD or contracts, and trend vs last week — rising OI = new money entering, falling OI = position liquidation - Any major institutional adoption or rejection news in the last 48 hours (e.g., corporate treasury buys, sovereign wealth fund exposure, bank custody announcements, major fund allocation) - Is "smart money" (ETF + CME positioning) net bullish or net bearish right now? - Distinguish between: (a) ETF flows as a demand signal, and (b) CME OI as a leverage/positioning signal ⚠ STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULE: Do NOT fabricate ETF flow figures. If confirmed data is unavailable for a specific day, state "Data Unavailable" explicitly. 
💵 STABLECOIN MARKET CAP TREND & MINTING/BURNING FLOWS (MANDATORY FOR CRYPTO): Fetch the latest stablecoin supply data from a reliable source (CoinGecko, DefiLlama stablecoins dashboard, Tether transparency report, or Circle USDC attestations). Report: (1) Total stablecoin market cap (USDT + USDC + others, combined in USD billions) and the 7-day trend — Expanding or Contracting? (2) Tether (USDT) supply change in the last 48 hours — any large mint events (new USDT issued = fresh capital entering crypto = bullish signal) or large redemption/burn events (USDT redeemed = capital exiting crypto = bearish signal)? State the approximate size in USD millions if available. (3) USDC supply change in the last 48 hours — minting or burning? LOGIC: Expanding stablecoin market cap, particularly large USDT mint events, historically precedes buying pressure in BTC, ETH, and major altcoins — it signals that institutions or large traders are depositing fiat and preparing to buy crypto. Contracting stablecoin supply signals capital is leaving the crypto ecosystem — bearish. ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate mint/burn figures. State 'Data Unavailable' if exact 48h flows cannot be confirmed from a cited source. Output: - Total Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X billion] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting/Flat] (Source: [domain]) - USDT 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Tether Transparency / CoinGecko) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - USDC 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Circle / DefiLlama) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - Combined Stablecoin Flow Signal: [Bullish — capital entering / Bearish — capital leaving / Neutral / Mixed] Add to compressed summary block: - Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X B] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting] | USDT Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | USDC Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - BTC ETF Net Flow (last 48h): [+/- $X million] | Trend: [Accelerating Inflows / Decelerating / Outflows] - Top ETF by Flow: [issuer + amount] - CME BTC Futures OI: [level] | vs Last Week: [Rising/Falling/Flat] - Key Institutional News: [top headline + directional implication, or "None"] - Smart Money Net Stance: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Institutional Bias for BTC: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P2 - INSTITUTIONAL & ETF FLOWS] - BTC ETF Net Flow (48h): [+/- $X million] | Trend: [Accelerating/Decelerating/Outflows] - Top ETF Flow: [issuer + amount] - CME Futures OI: [level] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] - Key Institutional News: [headline or "None"] - Smart Money Stance: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] - Core Logic: [what ETF flows + CME OI together signal for BTC direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

(B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report: - Exchange Net Flow: is Bitcoin moving INTO exchanges (potential sell pressure — bearish) or OUT of exchanges to cold storage (accumulation — bullish)? Provide the approximate net flow direction and magnitude if available. - Bitcoin Hashrate: current level (EH/s), recent trend (rising/falling), and what it signals about miner confidence and network security - Miner Selling Pressure: are miners currently accumulating BTC or distributing/selling? Reference the Miner Position Index (MPI) or miner wallet outflow data if available. High miner selling = supply-side pressure. - Key On-Chain Valuation Levels: · MVRV Z-Score: current value — is BTC overvalued (>3.5), fairly valued, or undervalued (<0)? · Realized Price: current BTC Realized Price level — is spot price above or below? (Trading above Realized Price = on-chain profit; below = aggregate loss) - Any notable on-chain anomalies (whale accumulation, long-term holder selling, dormant coins moving) ⚠ STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULE: Do NOT fabricate on-chain metrics. State "Data Unavailable" if exact figures cannot be confirmed from live sources. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Exchange Net Flow: [Inflow/Outflow — approx. amount] | Signal: [Bullish Accumulation / Bearish Distribution / Neutral] - Hashrate: [EH/s] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] | Miner Confidence: [High/Low/Neutral] - Miner Selling Pressure: [High/Medium/Low] | Source: [MPI or wallet data] - MVRV Z-Score: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Zone: [Overvalued/Fair/Undervalued] - Realized Price: [$level] | Spot vs Realized: [Above/Below] | Signal: [In Profit/In Loss] - On-Chain Anomaly: [notable event or "None"] - On-Chain Bias for BTC: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ON-CHAIN HEALTH & SUPPLY] - Exchange Net Flow: [Inflow/Outflow] | Signal: [Accumulation/Distribution/Neutral] - Hashrate: [EH/s] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] - Miner Selling Pressure: [High/Medium/Low] - MVRV Z-Score: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Zone: [Overvalued/Fair/Undervalued] - Realized Price: [$level] | Spot: [Above/Below] - Key Anomaly: [event or "None"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [what the on-chain data signals about supply/demand health for BTC today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest Bitcoin sentiment and derivatives positioning data from sources like Alternative.me, Coinglass, or similar platforms. Fetch and report: - Crypto Fear & Greed Index: current score (0–100) and reading (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed). Note: Extreme Fear can be a contrarian buy signal; Extreme Greed can signal over-extension. - Perpetual Futures Funding Rate: current annualized funding rate across major exchanges (e.g., Binance, Bybit). Is the rate positive (longs paying shorts = bullish positioning, but risk of long squeeze) or negative (shorts paying longs = bearish positioning, but risk of short squeeze)? - Open Interest (Perp + Futures combined) direction vs price: is OI rising with price (healthy trend) or diverging (potential reversal signal)? - Recent Liquidation Data (last 24h): total long liquidations vs short liquidations in USD. Which side has been squeezed more? Are there any notable liquidation clusters ahead on the heatmap? - Contrarian Analysis: if Funding Rate is heavily positive AND Fear & Greed is in Extreme Greed, flag as a potential long-squeeze risk. If Funding Rate is heavily negative AND Fear & Greed is in Extreme Fear, flag as a potential short-squeeze setup. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear BTC/USD institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Macro & Liquidity (The Fiat Engine): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Institutional & ETF Flows: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. On-Chain Health & Supply Dynamics: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Derivatives Positioning: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Geopolitics & Regulatory Risk: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity (e.g., US ETF market open, macro data releases, Asia accumulation hours).

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian / Squeeze Play]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason — e.g., "US Open 13:30 UTC — ETF flow data confirms institutional demand"]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[BTC/USD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian / Squeeze Play]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```
phase7_telegram: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Using the research data below, generate ONLY the Telegram post in the exact format shown. No extra text, no commentary, no section headers beyond what is specified. Research Data: [1] Macro & Liquidity: [P1_OUT1] [2] ETF & Institutional Flows: [P1_OUT2] [3] On-Chain Health: [P1_OUT3] [4] Sentiment & Derivatives: [P1_OUT4] [5] Geopolitics & Regulatory Risk: [P1_OUT5] Master Bias: [FUND_BIAS_PLACEHOLDER] Output this exact format — fill in every bracket, nothing else: BTC/USD Fundamental Bias Look [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Today Confidence Level: [X]/10 Key Reasons Explain: 1. Macro & Liquidity: [One sentence — Fed rate cut probability, DXY direction, and what the fiat liquidity environment means for BTC] 2. ETF & Institutional Flows: [One sentence — net ETF inflows/outflows and CME OI trend, and what smart money is signaling] 3. On-Chain Health: [One sentence — exchange net flow direction, hashrate trend, and whether on-chain data confirms accumulation or distribution] 4. Sentiment & Derivatives: [One sentence — Fear & Greed Index reading, Funding Rate bias, and whether a contrarian squeeze setup exists] 5. Geopolitics & Regulatory Risk: [One sentence — top regulatory event, Gold safe-haven signal, Nasdaq correlation, and whether it supports or threatens the bias]
phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Crypto Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's BTC/USD fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions. Language Rule: Write the post in engaging Bengali (Bangla script), but you MUST keep all technical financial terms (e.g., Bullish, Bearish, ETF Inflows, On-chain, Funding Rate, Liquidation, Hashrate, Realized Price, MVRV, DXY, Open Interest, Long Squeeze, Short Squeeze, Fear and Greed Index, Risk-On, Risk-Off, Confidence Level) strictly in English. Do not translate trading or crypto terms into Bengali. Research Data: [1] Macro & Liquidity: [P1_OUT1] [2] ETF & Institutional Flows: [P1_OUT2] [3] On-Chain Health: [P1_OUT3] [4] Sentiment & Derivatives: [P1_OUT4] [5] Geopolitics & Regulatory Risk: [P1_OUT5] Master Bias: [FUND_BIAS_PLACEHOLDER] Format the Blog Post exactly with these sections: # ₿ [Generate a catchy title mixing English financial terms and Bangla] **📅 Date:** [CURRENT_DATE] ## 📝 Introduction (Market Overview) [Write 2–3 sentences in Bangla giving an overview of today's market mood and the overall BTC/USD directional bias drawn from the Master Bias] ## 🔍 The 5-Pillar Fundamental Breakdown Explain each pillar in detail in Bangla. Include exact data figures and explain the LOGIC — why each factor matters for BTC/USD today: 1. **Macro & Liquidity (The Fiat Engine):** [Detailed explanation — include Fed cut probability, exact DXY level and direction, US 10Y yield trend, Global M2 expansion/contraction status, and why fiat liquidity conditions are a primary driver of BTC price direction] 2. **Institutional & ETF Flows:** [Detailed explanation — include exact ETF net flow figures (inflow/outflow $), top issuer by volume, CME Futures OI trend, any major institutional news, and what smart money positioning implies for BTC] 3. **On-Chain Health & Supply Dynamics:** [Detailed explanation — include exchange net flow direction (accumulation vs distribution), Hashrate level and trend, miner selling pressure, MVRV Z-Score zone, Realized Price level vs spot, and what the combined on-chain data says about supply/demand health] 4. **Sentiment & Derivatives Positioning:** [Detailed explanation — include exact Fear & Greed Index score and reading, Funding Rate level (positive/negative, longs/shorts paying), OI vs price health, 24h liquidation data for longs vs shorts, and whether a contrarian Squeeze setup exists] 5. **Geopolitics & Regulatory Risk:** [Detailed explanation — include any SEC/CFTC actions and their impact, government wallet movements and supply risk, Gold safe-haven correlation to BTC bias, Nasdaq risk-on/off mode, BTC-Nasdaq correlation status, and overall risk environment rating] ## ⚖️ Final Verdict & Trading Bias [Conclude in Bangla with the final directional bias, the exact Confidence Level score, the Optimal Trading Window (e.g., wait for US market open due to ETF flow data, or Asia session shows accumulation), a clear explanation of what traders should watch for today, and the specific Invalidation Point that would cancel this bias] IMPORTANT OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY a complete, valid, standalone HTML page. Start with <!DOCTYPE html> and end with </html>. Include all CSS inline inside a <style> tag in the <head>. Do NOT include any markdown, backticks, or commentary outside the HTML. The page should be clean, modern, and mobile-friendly.
